bp_title: Implement solutions to mitigate the risk of system overload
question_stem: GENOPS02 — How do you monitor and manage operational health for your generative AI workload? (BP page re-fetched + verified 2026-06-23)
verbatim_text: GENOPS02-BP03 Implement solutions to mitigate the risk of system overload. Step 4 — Design retry logic: Implement idempotent operations where possible to facilitate safe retries; use AWS Step Functions for managing complex retry workflows; consider circuit breaker patterns for failing fast in case of repeated failures. (Full BP: scale inference / rate limiting + throttling / backoff / retry logic with idempotent operations / continuous monitoring. AWS risk High. Verified 2026-06-23 from genops02-bp03.html.)
outcome_note: PROMOTED 2026-06-23 -> GO2B3-01 (Make every side-effecting tool call safe to retry). 25th standard; FIRST from GENOPS02 (focus area reopened from CLOSED/0); new focus_area P12 - Operational Health Management. Anchors step 4 idempotency verbatim; BP's rate-limiting/throttling thrust stays not_promoted (GR3B1-01 + base-WAF REL05 + GENREL01). Flagged during the GR3B2-01 walk as the not-yet-authored idempotency standard both GR3B1-01 and GR3B2-01 defer to. User chose GO2B3 (Op-Ex, verbatim anchor) over GR3B2-02/GR3B1-02 (Reliability). Contract: per tool binding side_effecting + idempotency_key | idempotent:false; central harness attaches key by construction + bars unsafe auto-retry; 3 pre-merge lints (declaration-completeness, no-unsafe-retry, routed-execution AST). TELEMETRY DELIBERATELY NOT MANDATED (user-directed) - provider-honouring gap is an accepted documented limit (first such in catalogue), not a quarterly telemetry gate. step_promotion 3/3/3/3. impact High; agentic mandatory; foundational; enterprise tier. framework_mappings: aws + eu_ai_act Art 15 robustness (v1.0.1, unverified, control_ref null). aigp/nist omitted - aigp has no natural BoK v2.1 competency for retry-safety (same as GR3B1-01/GR3B2-01 cleared in the 2026-06-23 audit); nist unpopulated catalogue-wide. eu_ai_act added after user pushback: Art 15(4) resilience-to-faults is genuinely discharged by the idempotency gate (discharge test, not sibling-consistency). GR3B1-01/GR3B2-01 likely warrant the same - reliability-family EU AI Act pass logged as follow-up. RI at data/ri/GO2B3-01/README.md. principles.json parses, 25 standards, format_version 1.13 unchanged. GR3B1-01 + GR3B2-01 -> v1.0.2 (reciprocal change_history notes).
open_items: S3 re-upload + frontend build (24 -> 25). Reciprocal gate-text relaxation in GR3B2-01/GR3B1-01 (allow auto-retry when all tools idempotent) - note added, lint text not rewritten. GENREL05 + GENREL06 still to walk to close Reliability.
next_candidates: GENREL05 (Distributed availability) - next. Then GENREL06 (Distributed compute tasks).
